introLines.text: Vern, my whole family saw it. My wife, my kids, my mother-in-law. We all saw the same thing.
We were having a barbecue in the backyard when it happened. Six of us, all looking at the same sky.
I never believed in this stuff before tonight. My nine-year-old asked me what it was. I didn't have an answer.

detailLines.text: Bright white light, bigger than any star. Hovered above the trees for maybe three minutes. Then it split into two.
My daughter started crying. Not because she was scared - she said it was beautiful. Then it just... disappeared.
Complete silence in the neighborhood after. Like even the crickets stopped. Everyone just stood there.

defenseLines.text: Six people, Vern. Three generations. We all saw the exact same thing. You can't dismiss that.
I'm an accountant. I deal in facts. What I saw tonight doesn't fit any fact I know.
My kids don't lie to me. My wife doesn't lie to me. We all saw it. That's not a hallucination.

acceptanceLines.text: Thank you. We needed to hear that. The kids especially needed to know they're not crazy.
I'm going to tell them what you said. It'll help them sleep tonight.
My wife is nodding. She's listening too. We both feel better.

extraDetailLines.text: My wife got fifteen seconds on her phone before it vanished. It's shaky, but you can clearly see it split in two.
The neighbor came over after. He saw it too from his porch. Asked if we knew what it was. We didn't.
My mother-in-law is 72 and doesn't believe in anything supernatural. She's been quiet all night. Just... processing.

conclusionLines.text: Thanks Vern. I'm gonna go hug my kids now. Keep up the good work.
We'll never forget tonight. And we'll never stop looking up.
I'll try to send you my wife's video. People should see it. Thank you.
